task_completion.description: Open and read the risk stratification report
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of /home/ga/Documents/risk_stratification_report.txt, showing patient names and their clinical risk data.
The LibreHealth EHR main dashboard or calendar view is visible, indicating a successful login as admin.

task_completion.description: Add the clinical risk problem for the first patient
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient chart or 'Issues'/'Medical Problems' list for the first patient (high cardiovascular risk / ASCVD) shows the new clinical risk problem added to their record.

task_completion.description: Schedule a follow-up appointment for the first patient
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreHealth calendar or appointment scheduling screen shows a newly created appointment block for the first patient.

task_completion.description: Add the clinical risk problem for the second patient
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient chart or 'Issues'/'Medical Problems' list for the second patient (uncontrolled diabetes / high HbA1c) shows the new clinical risk problem added to their record.

task_completion.description: Schedule a follow-up appointment for the second patient
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreHealth calendar or appointment scheduling screen shows a newly created appointment block for the second patient.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the LibreHealth EHR web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating the LibreHealth EHR web GUI in a browser to add problems and schedule appointments, rather than using terminal scripts or API calls.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the EHR by modifying the database directly
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing MySQL commands or direct database insertions to update patient records.

integrity.description: The agent used the provided risk stratification report
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent actually opened the /home/ga/Documents/risk_stratification_report.txt file to retrieve the patient names and conditions, rather than guessing or using a pre-computed script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task involves two specific patients identified in the dynamically generated risk stratification report. Patient 1 has an ASCVD 10-year score of 32% (indicating High Cardiovascular Risk). Patient 2 has an HbA1c of 11.2% (indicating Uncontrolled Diabetes). The agent must add these specific clinical risk problems to their respective medical problem lists in LibreHealth EHR and schedule follow-up appointments for both. The exact patient names are found in the text report.
